items.title: Arctic Winter Games kick off in Whitehorse
What we know about Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei
items.summary_auto: Iran on Monday named Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father, Ali Khamenei, as supreme leader, signalling that hardliners remain firmly in charge in Tehran more than a week ‌into its conflict with the United States and Israel.

items.title: Damaging winds leave some Albertans without power as some weather warnings remain in place
items.summary_auto: Environment Canada is warning residents in parts of Alberta to secure loose objects and expect damage as strong winds whip through the region, while leaving thousands of residents in the dark.

items.title: Bystanders help save two youth who fell through ice, Hamilton police say
items.summary_auto: An officer with Hamilton police's marine unit says no ice is safe after bystanders helped rescue two youth who fell through the ice near the Pier 7 boardwalk.

items.title: 2 arrested after explosive device thrown at protest near Mayor Mamdani's house: NYPD
items.summary_auto: New York ​City police said on Sunday a device that was ignited and thrown during protests outside Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home over the weekend was an explosive that could have caused serious injury or death.

items.title: Ontario police rescue 23 people stranded on ice shelf that separated from shore in Georgian Bay
items.summary_auto: Provincial police say 23 people were rescued after a piece of ice they were standing on separated from shore in Georgian Bay.

items.title: Ontario police rescue 23 people stranded on ice shelf that separated from shore
items.summary_auto: Provincial police say 23 people were rescued after a piece of ice they were standing on separated from shore in Georgian Bay.

items.title: As war takes its toll in Iran, some still hope for regime change
items.summary_auto: Iran's ambassador to the United Nations said 1,332 Iranian civilians have been killed in the conflict so far. With the war now in its second week, some opponents of Iran's governing regime are conflicted by the U.S.-Israeli attacks on their country.

items.title: Sask. trained Kurt Oatway takes bronze in Paralympic skiing
items.summary_auto: Kurt Oatway, the 2018 Paralympic gold medal winner who trained in Saskatchewan has just medalled again, taking bronze in men's downhill sit-skiing.

items.title: Canadian Para alpine star Mollie Jepsen to miss rest of Milano-Cortina Games with knee injury
items.summary_auto: Mollie Jepsen's Paralympics have come to an early end due to a knee injury. The Canadian Paralympic Committee and Alpine Canada announced that the West Vancouver, B.C., native will not compete in the four events remaining on her schedule.

items.title: Anxiety in some Alberta mountain towns as wildfire season begins
items.summary_auto: Despite spring snow still dusting parts of Alberta, it's now officially wildfire season, and with it comes once again the gnawing worry about which community will next be touched by nature's fickle finger of destruction.

items.title: Roaring crowd sets new Paralympic hockey attendance record at U.S.-Italy game in Milan
items.summary_auto: The opening Para ice hockey match at the Milano-Cortina Winter Games set an attendance record for the sport at a major tournament.

items.title: Roaring crowd of 8,992 sets Para hockey attendance record at U.S.-Italy game in Milan
items.summary_auto: The opening Para ice hockey match at the Milano-Cortina Winter Games set an attendance record for the sport at a major tournament.

items.title: Former rapper and Gen Z's candidate poised to win Nepali election by landslide
items.summary_auto: In a resounding victory that is guaranteed to reshape Nepal's politics, rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah appeared headed for a landslide win in the South Asian country's first polls since youth-led protests toppled the previous government last fall.

items.title: 'Shaken to our core': Jewish community urges action against antisemitism after GTA synagogue shootings
items.summary_auto: Jewish community members are calling on the Canadian government and residents to take a stance against hate and antisemitism after three synagogues were hit by gunfire this week in the Greater Toronto Area.

items.title: Carney calls byelections in Quebec riding of Terrebonne, 2 Toronto ridings
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney has called byelections in the Toronto ridings of University-Rosedale and Scarborough Southwest and the Quebec riding of Terrebonne — races the Liberals will want to win to secure a thin majority government.

items.title: Iran names Khamenei's son Mojtaba as new supreme leader: state media
Alberta and the U.S. have been arguing over electricity — but a ‘win-win’ may be in sight
items.summary_auto: After months of complaints from Montana politicians and scrutiny from U.S. trade officials, Alberta’s utilities minister says he recently had a great chat with a Montana legislator who has been critical of the province’s approach to electricity.

items.title: Northern Manitoba mobile MRI unit parked in Thompson, struggling to keep staff, associations say
items.summary_auto: A mobile MRI unit intended to serve patients in two northern Manitoba communities has been parked in Thompson and is struggling to retain staff, according to two associations representing technologists.

items.title: Canadian company seeks to stand out with domestically-made generic Ozempic
items.summary_auto: As geopolitical uncertainties persist, the possibility of a Canadian-made version of one of the most popular drugs in the world is prompting conversations about drug manufacturing capabilities in this country.

items.title: Greenlanders should still worry about Trump's designs on their island, Governor General says
items.summary_auto: Although U.S. President Donald Trump's interest in taking over Greenland appears to have faded — at least publicly — Canada's Governor General says concern remains that the fight over the semi-autonomous territory isn't over yet.

items.title: Millions of Americans can now claim Canadian citizenship by descent. But they have to prove it
items.summary_auto: Amid rising tensions in the United States and increased global volatility, many Americans are looking to Canada — and their roots — for a possible way out following recent changes to Canada’s citizenship rules.

items.title: SpaceX wants to launch a million satellites. Here's how that could impact the atmosphere and the night sky
items.summary_auto: Scientists are deeply concerned about SpaceX's recent proposal to launch one million satellites into orbit around Earth. Their concerns range from a loss of the natural night sky and our access to space, to the environmental impact on our atmosphere.

items.title: Keeping up the royal appearances amid the Andrew scandal
items.summary_auto: Even though senior members of the Royal Family haven't been commenting publicly about former prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, in keeping up their regular appearances they have been sending messages amid the controversy swirling around King Charles’s younger brother.

items.title: U.S. customs searched a record number of electronic devices last year
items.summary_auto: United States customs officers conducted a record number of searches of electronic devices last year of people travelling to the U.S., and a recent update to its directives adds new devices such as smart watches, SIM cards and flash drives to the list of things subject to search.

items.title: At 25, Wikipedia doubles down on its 'human-created, human-vetted' philosophy
items.summary_auto: It's been 25 years since Wikipedia first launched, but what does its future look like in a world where traditional media is on the decline and AI is on the rise?

items.title: Jays pitcher Max Scherzer is a rare bird, still flying high as a competitive player in his 40s
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Blue Jays have brought pitcher Max Scherzer back into the fold for another run at a World Series title in 2026. It's a rare case of a player wearing a Jays jersey past age 40 — or in Scherzer's case, doing so for the second year in a row.

items.title: Critics say Ottawa's sustainable jobs plan offers no new support for energy workers
items.summary_auto: The Carney government's plan to protect jobs in the energy sector amounts to a report that critics say offers no new actions for workers who are at risk of losing work.

items.title: At 25, Wikipedia faces a double threat: the rise of AI and the decline of local media
items.summary_auto: It's been 25 years since Wikipedia first launched, but what does its future look like in a world where traditional media is on the decline and AI is on the rise?
